title: lessingia glomerata
intro: Lessingia glomerata, also known as clustered lessingia or clustered sea lavender, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Lessingia glomerata can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which are often used to treat a range of conditions, from minor wounds to more serious infections.
